finding.summary: Track labelled 'Distorted Guitar Alt' shows a crest factor of 12.0 dB and an integrated loudness of -20.0 LUFS, with the single Drive effect sitting at a moderate normalized 0.5 (formatted 5.0). For a track explicitly intended to be distorted, this crest factor is on the high side — heavily driven guitar tones typically land closer to 6–9 dB crest factor — suggesting the saturation stage is not fully committing to the role the name implies. The 1/3-octave snapshot shows a strongly descending tilt (100 Hz at -18.0 dB, 1 kHz at -24.0 dB, 4 kHz at -30.0 dB); with only three bands reported this cannot be diagnosed precisely, but the 6–12 dB low-to-high slope is consistent with a relatively dark, under-driven guitar that hasn't yet generated the upper-harmonic stack distortion usually produces. Silence fraction is low (0.05), so the level and crest statistics are not diluted by dead air and can be trusted.
finding.probable_cause: The Drive parameter is set to a moderate value (normalized 0.5 / formatted 5.0 of what appears to be a 0–10 scale). At this setting the saturation is producing some harmonic content but not enough soft-knee compression of the waveform peaks to bring the crest factor into the range typically associated with a 'distorted' guitar part. The descending spectrum tilt (100 Hz strongest, 4 kHz weakest) reinforces the read that the signal is dynamic and bass-weighted rather than dense and saturated.
finding.confidence_reason: The crest-factor and drive-setting mismatch is directly evidenced by the FX parameter and the post-FX audio snapshot, and is corroborated by the spectral tilt. However, the artistic intent of a 12 dB crest factor is genre- and context-dependent — a player may want a 'distorted' guitar that still breathes — so this is a style call as much as a technical one. Only three 1/3-octave bands are available, so detailed tonal diagnosis (e.g., specific resonance or mud) cannot be supported.

evidence_refs.description: Track name explicitly identifies the part as 'Distorted'
Track volume at 0 dB, no offset
Single active plugin is a Drive effect
True peak -5.5 dB, 0.5 dB above sample peak, no inter-sample risk
Crest factor 12.0 dB, high for a 'distorted' guitar

proposal.reason: The proposed normalized FX parameter change was rejected by deterministic validation.
